text.title: Capacity-Fit Calculator Failure Modes
text.summary: Five failure modes that quietly inflate the capacity number and cause sprint over-commitment, especially when an LLM is generating the sheet.

failure-mode.description: Ceremonies under-counted — only standup is subtracted, planning/refinement/retro forgotten.
failure-mode.repair: Inventory all ceremonies and meeting holds for the sprint. Typical floor for a Scrum team: 5-8h per person per 2-week sprint.

failure-mode.description: PTO calendar not pulled — assumes full headcount across all working days.
failure-mode.detector: pto_hours = 0 across > 50% of sprints in capacity-sheet AND team is > 3 engineers
failure-mode.repair: Make PTO check a hard step in the pre-planning checklist. Pull from HR system or shared calendar. Zero-PTO weeks exist but are not the average.

failure-mode.description: Interrupt buffer set to 0 because "we're shielded this sprint."
failure-mode.detector: interrupt_buffer_pct = 0 AND no documented separate on-call rotation
failure-mode.repair: Set buffer to default 15% minimum. The "shielded" assumption is consistently falsified. If genuinely on-call-isolated, document it once in capacity-sheet notes and reference each sprint.

failure-mode.description: Velocity baseline cherry-picked — highest of last 3 sprints used.
failure-mode.detector: velocity_baseline equals the max of the last 3 sprints >= 2 times in 6 sprints
failure-mode.repair: Use rolling-3-sprint median or mean, not max. Discuss why one sprint was an outlier instead of treating it as the new normal.

failure-mode.description: Story points converted to hours with an inflated conversion factor.
failure-mode.detector: committed_velocity in hours is > story_points × 6 (typical conversion 4-6h/point depending on team)
failure-mode.repair: Calibrate conversion from actual logged hours per point over the last 3 sprints. Avoid mixing point-based commitment with hour-based capacity unless the conversion is empirically stable.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: LLM-generated capacity sheets must not invent terms. If asked to "estimate ceremonies" with no input, the model must return UNKNOWN and prompt the PM, not fabricate 2h/person. Calculator output without grounded inputs is forbidden.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: RED-band sprints require a named human sign-off line ("Risk accepted by [PM name] on [date] because [reason]"). The calculator can recommend the trim list; it cannot grant the risk acceptance.
